body.response:
Name: Yamini Singh
Age: 33 years old
Occupation: Unemployed, was previously attached in a MNC
Company Sales Team
Marital Status: Separated, Divorced
Family: Father (deceased, alcoholic), Sister (deceased),
Mother(schizophrenia, liver cirrhosis)
Education: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science from a
prestigious university
Residence: Lives alone in a duplex apartment in a
metropolitan city

Yamini mentioned in her early discussion, that she had bad
some major episodes on,

2020: Severe depression following the sudden death of her
brother. She was hospitalised for six weeks and prescribed
Fluoxetine (Prozac) and Olanzapine (Zyprexa). During this
time, she also began seeing a therapist for grief
counselling.
2016: Experienced a manic episode characterised by
excessive energy, decreased need for sleep, and impulsive
decision-making. Hospitalised for three weeks and diagnosed
with Bipolar Disorder. Treated with Lithium and Valproate.
2015: Another major depressive episode triggered by the
anniversary of her brother's death. She was hospitalised
for four weeks, during which her medications were adjusted,
and she received intensive psychotherapy. Her treatment
included a brief course of inpatient Electroconvulsive
Therapy (ECT).

Current Hospitalisation:
Reason for Admission: Yamini was admitted following a
suicide attempt involving an overdose of her prescribed
medications. She has been experiencing worsening symptoms
of depression and anxiety, including severe insomnia, loss
of appetite, weight loss, pervasive feelings of
hopelessness, and frequent panic attacks.
Length of Stay: Currently in her third week of
hospitalisation in a psychiatric unit.

Current Medications:
Fluoxetine (Prozac), Sertraline (Zoloft) Oral, after food
twice daily for 6 months
Lithium, Valproate Oral, with milk once daily for 6 months
Olanzapine (Zyprexa) Oral, after food Once daily for 20

days
Lorazepam (Ativan) Oral, after food twice daily for 1 year

Medication on 2020:
Aripiprazole (Abilify) - 5 mg daily oral once after food
Zolpidem (Ambien) - 5 mg as needed once everyday, with milk
orally

Medication on 2017:
Escitalopram (Lexapro) - 20 mg daily Oral, after food Once
daily for 10 days
Lamotrigine (Lamictal) - 200 mg daily Oral, after food once
a week for 3 weeks

Medication on 2015:
Aripiprazole (Abilify) - 10 mg daily oral for 30 days
Clonazepam (Klonopin) - 0.5 mg oral once in a week after
food
Zolpidem (Ambien) - 10 mg once everyday, with milk orally
for 3 months

Past Therapies:
2017 - Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) focusing on
managing depressive and anxious thoughts
2016-2017 - Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT) for emotion
regulation and distress tolerance
2020 - Group Therapy sessions for social support and shared
experiences
Grief counselling after her brother's death

Current Therapies:
Intensive CBT focusing on restructuring negative thought
patterns and developing coping skills
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) to manage stress
and anxiety
Individual Psychotherapy sessions once a week with a focus
on processing grief and trauma
Occupational Therapy to improve daily functioning and work-
life balance

Father: Alcoholic, passed away at age 45 from liver
cirrhosis. His alcoholism led to an unstable and abusive
home environment during Yamini's childhood, contributing to
Yamini's early experiences with anxiety and depression.
Brother: Died in a car accident when Yamini was 18. The

loss was a significant traumatic event that triggered
Yamini's first major depressive episode.

Psychosocial Factors:
Social Support: Has a close-knit group of friends and
colleagues, but her relationships are often strained due to
her mental health issues and demanding work schedule. She
finds it difficult to open up about her struggles.
Hobbies and Interests: Enjoys reading, playing the guitar,
and hiking. However, she has neglected these activities
over the past year due to her deteriorating mental health.
Substance Use: Occasionally consumes alcohol socially, but
has avoided excessive drinking due to her father's history
of alcoholism.

Current Treatment Goals:
Stabilise Mood: Achieve mood stabilisation through a
combination of medication and psychotherapy.
Reduce Symptoms: Alleviate symptoms of depression and
anxiety to improve daily functioning.
Improve Sleep: Address insomnia through medication and
behavioural interventions.
Enhance Coping Mechanisms: Develop healthier coping
strategies to manage stress and emotional triggers.
Improve Work-Life Balance: Encourage a healthier balance
between work and personal life to reduce burnout.
Family Therapy: Consider family therapy sessions to address
unresolved issues related to her father and brother's
deaths.
Reintegration Plan: Develop a structured plan for
reintegration into her daily life and work, ensuring
support systems are in place to prevent relapse.

Prognosis:
Yamini's prognosis is cautiously optimistic. She adheres to
treatment and actively engages in therapeutic
interventions. Regular follow-ups, a robust support system,
and a balanced approach to work and self-care are essential
for sustained improvement.

Current Vitals:

Vitals:
Height: 5 feet 10 inches (178 cm)
Weight: 150 pounds (68 kg)
Body Mass Index (BMI): 21.5 (Normal weight)
Blood Pressure: 130/85 mmHg (Slightly elevated)

Heart Rate: 78 beats per minute (Normal)
Respiratory Rate: 16 breaths per minute (Normal)
Temperature: 98.6°F (37°C) (Normal)
Oxygen Saturation: 98% on room air (Normal)

Laboratory Results:

Current Lab Tests:
Complete Blood Count (CBC): Within normal limits
Liver Function Tests (LFTs): Slightly elevated ALT (likely
due to medication)
Renal Function: Normal
Thyroid Function Tests: Normal
Electrolytes: Within normal limits
Lipid Profile: Slightly elevated LDL cholesterol

Psychiatric Assessment:

Current Mental Status Exam:
Appearance: Well-groomed but appearing fatigued
Behaviour: Cooperative but exhibits psychomotor retardation
Speech: Slow and soft
Mood: Depressed
Affect: Constricted
Thought Process: Logical but slowed
Thought Content: Expresses feelings of hopelessness and
worthlessness, denies current suicidal ideation
Cognition: Intact orientation to time, place, and person;
attention and concentration mildly impaired
Insight and Judgment: Good insight into her condition,
judgment intact but affected by mood

Interventions for Yamini Singh

Medication Management:
Fluoxetine (Prozac)
Sertraline (Zoloft)
Lithium
Valproate
Olanzapine (Zyprexa)
Lorazepam (Ativan)
Escitalopram (Lexapro)
Lamotrigine (Lamictal)
Aripiprazole (Abilify)
Clonazepam (Klonopin)
Zolpidem (Ambien)

Psychotherapy:
Intensive Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT)
Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT)
Individual Psychotherapy
Group Therapy
Grief Counseling

Behavioural Interventions:
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR)
Occupational Therapy

Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT):
Brief inpatient course during a major depressive episode in
2015

Hospitalisation:
Multiple admissions for severe depressive and manic
episodes
Current hospitalisation following a suicide attempt

Supportive Therapies:
Family Therapy (consideration for future intervention)

Lifestyle and Daily Functioning:
Developing a structured plan for reintegration into daily
life and work
Encouragement of hobbies and interests (reading, playing
guitar, hiking) as part of a balanced lifestyle
